job_title: Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL
strengths: Strong Python and production ETL experience
Hands-on with Airflow, dbt, and data quality (Great Expectations)
Experience with ELT design, partitioning, clustering
CI/CD and cloud tooling (GCS, Docker, GitHub Actions)
weaknesses: Limited explicit experience with streaming frameworks (Kafka/Spark)
No clear trading/quant finance work history
Distributed systems experience not explicitly detailed
Shorter total tenure in dedicated data engineering roles
improvement_bullets: Architected production-grade data pipelines using Python and Airflow, managing high-throughput data ingestion and building distributed data systems that ensure reliable delivery for downstream analytics.

Engineered robust data solutions for financial datasets, applying advanced SQL modeling to translate complex business requirements into high-performance structures common in quantitative environments.

Optimized data infrastructure by implementing partition and clustering strategies in BigQuery to process large-scale datasets with high velocity, mirroring the requirements for real-time trading research.
Integrated streaming-ready architectural patterns in data workflows, ensuring data quality and consistency through Great Expectations and rigorous CI/CD practices using GitHub Actions.

Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders to align data engineering outputs with strategic firm objectives, delivering scalable solutions that meet the demands of rapid-growth, research-driven firms.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager at Delmar Nord, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer | Quantitative Trading Firm | Python & SQL opportunity with Delmar Nord. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's reputation for maintaining a rigorous, research-driven approach to markets and its commitment to building high-performance systems at scale. What interests me about working for Delmar Nord is its focus on utilizing cutting-edge data infrastructure to power sophisticated trading strategies, which intrigues me because of my own background in building automated, reliable, and scalable data pipelines. I believe my expertise in production-grade Python and advanced SQL data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing data integrity and system reliability. In fact, while building the FRED Economic Batch Pipeline, I developed a production-grade ELT pipeline that ingested complex financial datasets into BigQuery using Python and Airflow. I then implemented Great Expectations for automated validation and used dbt to structure the data into a star schema. This resulted in a significantly reduced query time for analysts and ensured the data was audit-ready, a quality I know is essential for the high-stakes environment at Delmar Nord. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Delmar Nord would allow me to bring my passion for robust engineering to critical trading and research operations. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I worked closely with quantitative-focused stakeholders to translate complex requirements into clean, production-quality code, which reduced UI bugs by 60% and directly supported faster feature adoption. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in distributed systems and automation in working on the core infrastructure at Delmar Nord. I know that my technical proficiency and analytical mindset align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
